title: Lab 1: LED Test
desc: Build a simple LED circuit and prove the hardware works

title: Lab 2: LED Control
desc: Control an LED from the MicroPython console using GPIO

title: Lab 3: LED Flashing
desc: Write a program that flashes an LED using while True and time.sleep

title: Lab 4: Switch Input
desc: Read a push button from the console and understand pull-up resistors

title: Lab 5: Switch and LED
desc: Write a program that lights an LED when a button is pressed

title: Lab 1: Pixel Test
desc: Set up a NeoPixel strand from the console and explore colour

title: Lab 2: Colour Range
desc: Write a program that displays a spread of colours across the strip

title: Lab 3: Palette and Button
desc: Define colour palettes and use a button to cycle between them

title: Lab 4: Lerp Fade
desc: Use linear interpolation to smoothly fade between colours

title: Lab 5: Non-Blocking Animation
desc: Use Python generators to run multiple animations with button switching

title: Lab 1: Software PWM
desc: Build a PWM signal in software and understand duty cycle

title: Lab 2: Hardware PWM
desc: Use the Pico's built-in PWM hardware for precise waveforms

title: Lab 3: L298N H-Bridge
desc: Connect a DC motor to the L298N and control direction and speed

title: Lab 4: Motor Driver Class
desc: Wrap the L298N control logic in a clean Python class

title: Lab 5: Button-Controlled Motor
desc: Use buttons to cycle motor speed and toggle direction

title: Lab 1: Servo PWM
desc: Understand the 50 Hz PWM signal that controls servo position

title: Lab 2: I2C Introduction
desc: Learn the I2C protocol and scan for devices on the bus

title: Lab 3: PCA9685 Registers
desc: Write directly to PCA9685 registers to move a servo

title: Lab 4: Servo Driver Class
desc: Build PCA9685 and ServoDriver classes with a clean angle() interface

title: Lab 5: Multi-Servo Control
desc: Control four servos independently using four buttons

title: Getting Started
desc: Install MicroPython and the CLB framework, then run a test program

title: Button Light
desc: Light up a NeoPixel strip when a button is pressed

title: Encoder Brightness
desc: Use a rotary encoder to control NeoPixel brightness
